curveRfreq 0.0.0.9000

    • Frequentist Modeling of Immunoassay Standard Curves with the StandardCurve Class: Bead Array Example

Compute MDC and RDL values from fitted model and LODs

Source: R/curve_diagnostics.R
generate_mdc_rdl.Rd

Computes minimum and maximum detectable concentrations (MDC) and reliable detection limits (RDL) by solving for concentrations where the fitted curve or its confidence bounds intersect the LODs.

Usage

generate_mdc_rdl(best_fit, lods, independent_variable, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

best_fit

A list containing:

best_fit

A fitted model object supporting predict(), coef(), and vcov().

best_data

A data.frame used for fitting the model.

lods

A list containing llod and ulod, typically from generate_lods.

independent_variable

Character string naming the independent variable column in best_data.

verbose

Logical; if TRUE, prints computed MDC and RDL values.

Value

A named list with:

mindc

Minimum detectable concentration (fitted curve = LLOD).

maxdc

Maximum detectable concentration (fitted curve = ULOD).

minrdl

Minimum reliable detection limit (lower CI = LLOD).

maxrdl

Maximum reliable detection limit (upper CI = ULOD).

Details

Root-finding is performed using uniroot() over the observed range of the independent variable.

The prediction standard error is computed using the delta method: $$SE(\hat{y}) = \sqrt{\nabla f(\theta)^T V \nabla f(\theta)}$$ where \(V\) is the variance-covariance matrix of the fitted parameters.

Reliable detection limits (RDL) are defined using the confidence interval:

  • Lower bound: \(\hat{y}(x) - t_{\alpha/2} \cdot SE(x)\)

  • Upper bound: \(\hat{y}(x) + t_{\alpha/2} \cdot SE(x)\)

If no valid root is found within the observed data range, the corresponding value is returned as NA.

See also

generate_lods

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
lods <- generate_lods(best_fit)
mdc_rdl <- generate_mdc_rdl(best_fit, lods, independent_variable = "log_dilution")
} # }
